session_hint: the Cumulative Layout Shift investigation on article pages
question: What did we rule out as causes of the high CLS score on the article pages?
turns: Our Lighthouse CLS score on article pages is 0.38, way over the 0.1 threshold. Initial suspicion: web fonts loading late and causing text to reflow before the page settles.
Added font-display: swap and preloaded the two web fonts with <link rel=preload>. CLS barely changed — dropped to only 0.35. Font loading is not the main culprit, ruling it out.

Checked all images: every tag has explicit width and height attributes set. The browser reserves space correctly. Layout Shift attribution in DevTools confirms image shift contribution is under 0.01. Images are not the issue.

Identified the real cause: a third-party ad iframe gets injected 800ms after page load by the ad SDK, pushing all article content below it down by 250px. Switching to a reserved ad slot with a fixed 250px height placeholder fixed the CLS score to 0.05.
